speaker: HELEN
text: That you may well perceive I have not wronged you,

speaker: HELEN
text: One of the greatest in the Christian world

speaker: HELEN
text: Shall be my surety, ’fore whose throne ’tis needful,

speaker: HELEN
text: Ere I can perfect mine intents, to kneel.

speaker: HELEN
text: Time was, I did him a desirèd office

speaker: HELEN
text: Dear almost as his life, which gratitude

speaker: HELEN
text: Through flinty Tartar’s bosom would peep forth

speaker: HELEN
text: And answer thanks. I duly am informed

speaker: HELEN
text: His Grace is at Marseilles, to which place

speaker: HELEN
text: We have convenient convoy. You must know

speaker: HELEN
text: I am supposèd dead. The army breaking,

speaker: HELEN
text: My husband hies him home, where, heaven aiding

speaker: HELEN
text: And by the leave of my good lord the King,

speaker: HELEN
text: We’ll be before our welcome.

speaker: WIDOW
text: Gentle madam,

speaker: WIDOW
text: You never had a servant to whose trust

speaker: WIDOW
text: Your business was more welcome.

speaker: HELEN
text: Nor you, mistress,

speaker: HELEN
text: Ever a friend whose thoughts more truly labor

speaker: HELEN
text: To recompense your love. Doubt not but heaven

speaker: HELEN
text: Hath brought me up to be your daughter’s dower,

speaker: HELEN
text: As it hath fated her to be my motive

speaker: HELEN
text: And helper to a husband. But O, strange men,

speaker: HELEN
text: That can such sweet use make of what they hate

speaker: HELEN
text: When saucy trusting of the cozened thoughts

speaker: HELEN
text: Defiles the pitchy night! So lust doth play

speaker: HELEN
text: With what it loathes for that which is away.

speaker: HELEN
text: But more of this hereafter.—You, Diana,

speaker: HELEN
text: Under my poor instructions yet must suffer

speaker: HELEN
text: Something in my behalf.

speaker: DIANA
text: Let death and honesty

speaker: DIANA
text: Go with your impositions, I am yours

speaker: DIANA
text: Upon your will to suffer.

speaker: HELEN
text: Yet, I pray you—

speaker: HELEN
text: But with the word “The time will bring on summer,”

speaker: HELEN
text: When briers shall have leaves as well as thorns

speaker: HELEN
text: And be as sweet as sharp. We must away.

speaker: HELEN
text: Our wagon is prepared, and time revives us.

speaker: HELEN
text: All’s well that ends well. Still the fine’s the crown.

speaker: HELEN
text: Whate’er the course, the end is the renown.
